findings.message: T001 claims to satisfy the `<layer>-types.json` schema migration, but narrows the catalogue migration to `domain-types.json` and `infrastructure-types.json` only. That conflicts with `spec.json` IN-02/CN-04, which require a schema-version update and an explicit migration policy for the layer catalogues as a breaking change; the scoped `usecase-types.json` remains a layer catalogue and is left out of the planned migration. Implementing this as written can land the v4 codec while a scoped catalogue is still planned to stay at schema v3.

T001 is marked as covering IN-05/AC-07/AC-11, but its implementation scope only removes SpecRef hash fields from the type/codec/verifier paths and updates those verifier fixtures; it never includes the Chain② pair source or a ref-verify/cache-staleness verification. As written, an implementer can complete T001 while leaving the plan's promised behavior unverified: pair_source_chain2 must continue producing claim_hash from the hash-free catalogue-entry JSON, and a spec edit must stale the verify-cache without any spec_refs[].hash transcription. Add that code/test coverage to T001 or stop mapping those criteria to it.

The spec requires the schema-breaking migration policy to be explicit for existing catalogues, either by migrating existing in-flight tracks or by declaring the break as new-catalogue-only, but T001 only says to migrate this track's own catalogue files to schema_version 4. That leaves the in-scope schema-version change without the required migration stance, so the task coverage for IN-02 is overstated.

CN-02 says SpecRef anchors must exist in `spec.json`'s `decisions[].id`, but SpecRef points at spec elements such as GO/IN/CN/AC IDs; `decisions[].id` is the ADR-frontmatter shape. This contradicts AC-03/AC-05/AC-06's requirement to validate anchors against actual spec elements and can send the implementation toward checking the wrong namespace for dangling anchors.

IN-08 requires the catalogue codec plan to explicitly define how hash-containing old schema inputs are handled, choosing either error or ignore, but T001 is the only mapped task and only says to remove hash from the DTO/codec, bump SCHEMA_VERSION, migrate existing catalogues, and update tests. An implementation can complete T001 exactly as written while leaving the required old-schema behavior undefined, so the IN-08 coverage claim is semantically incomplete.
